frameworks.name: uncertainty-directed exploration
frameworks.one_line: choices are biased toward options whose value is uncertain

frameworks.name: feature-based generalization
frameworks.one_line: value learned from features transfers to guide exploration of new options

frameworks.name: computational models of exploration
frameworks.one_line: lab-derived exploration models tested against real-world consumer choices

frameworks.name: adaptive exploration
frameworks.one_line: exploration level scales with environmental average quality

core_question: Do laboratory models of exploration and generalization explain choice in a large-scale real-world decision setting?
key_assumptions: choice value can be modeled from observed order history
lab exploration models extend to naturalistic decisions
manipulations: variation in average restaurant quality across cities
variation in option uncertainty within each customer's history
variation in shared features across restaurants
key_findings: exploration level is adjusted to a city's average restaurant quality
feature-based generalization steers exploration toward promising restaurants
interpretations: hallmarks of adaptive exploration generalize from lab tasks to real-world choice
computational exploration models capture naturalistic consumer behavior
